top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 81, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tartu Rattaralli's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
Tartu Rattaralli's flat-fast with rolling kicks rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Tartu Rattaralli's flat-fast with rolling kicks forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 81 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tartu Rattaralli. Train it until it feels boring.
At Tartu Rattaralli, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
When someone attacks on flat-fast with rolling kicks, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 81 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Tartu Rattaralli, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
The aerobic base that lets you ride flat-fast with rolling kicks at tempo for 81 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on flat-fast with rolling kicks. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 81 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.heat: June in Tartu, South Estonia, Estonia can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Tartu Rattaralli. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Tartu Rattaralli’s 81 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Tartu Rattaralli requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on flat-fast with rolling kicks to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 81 miles of flat-fast with rolling kicks in Tartu, South Estonia, Estonia.
